title: Limb Targeting Combat System
text: Fear & Hunger's combat revolves around targeting individual enemy limbs. Each limb has its own HP pool. Destroying limbs changes enemy behavior: destroying a cleaver arm removes cleaver attacks, destroying a stinger disables thrust attacks, destroying legs reduces mobility. Enemies can likewise dismember player characters — lost limbs are permanently gone without special restorative items. Strategic limb targeting is essential for survival. Dismemberment is a core part of combat for all characters.

title: Coin Flip Mechanic
text: Many enemies have a coin flip attack — an unavoidable instant-kill or massive-damage move on specific turns. Players must Guard on the coin flip turn to survive. Destroying the limb that enables the coin flip disables it (e.g., the Guard's right arm for Mad Rush). Coin flips have a 50% success rate. Understanding enemy coin flip patterns is critical — failure often means death. Coin flip turns follow predictable patterns (every 3 turns for Guards).

title: Saving and Permanent Death
text: The game can only be saved at ritual circles scattered throughout the dungeons. There is no automatic checkpointing. Death is permanent. Players must weigh the risk of pressing forward against the safety of backtracking to save. Some ritual circles are hidden or require specific conditions to access. This creates genuine tension — every decision carries weight.

title: No Leveling System — Stats
text: Fear & Hunger has no experience points or leveling. Character stats improve only through equipment, consumable items (Blue Vials for attack), or by interacting with entities like the Human Hydra with a Ring of Wraiths. This makes base stats critical and equipment choices life-or-death. Knowledge of the game's mechanics and world is the player's main leverage — the game rewards learning through failure.

title: Soul Types and Ascension
text: Characters possess distinct soul types with narrative significance: Cahara has the Endless soul (yearning for freedom), D'arce has the Domination soul (natural leadership), Enki has the Enlightened soul (pursuit of knowledge), Ragnvaldr has the Tormented soul (forged by suffering), the Girl has the Ancient One soul (divine potential). The New Gods each embody one soul type. Soul stones can be used on corpses to acquire souls. Le'garde's plan involves encompassing all soul types to transcend the limits of any single New God.

title: Hunger and Survival Mechanics
text: Hunger is a persistent mechanic — characters must eat food items or suffer increasing penalties. Starvation reduces stats and eventually kills. Food is scavenged from the environment, dropped by enemies, or bought from merchants. Some food can cause infection or poisoning. Mind represents mental stability — low mind triggers phobias, hallucinations, and combat penalties. Mind is restored through items like Tobacco, reading books, or resting in safe areas. Infection worsens over time if untreated and must be cured with Green Herbs.

title: Marriage and Fusion
text: At ritual circles, two party members can be merged into a Marriage — a powerful combined entity. A Marriage (Fusion) is a further evolution. Marriages cannot achieve character-specific S-endings. This is a permanent and irreversible choice. The Abominable Marriage is a variant with different implications. Buckman's party (Ser Seymor and companions) form a failed marriage in the Level 7 Catacombs if spoken to, marking the end of their journey.
